steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the width of the base
steps.content:
Base area = length × width, so:
$$24t = \frac{8}{3} \times w \implies w = 24t \times \frac{3}{8} = 9t \text{ cm}$$
Dimensions: l = 8/3 cm, w = 9t cm, h = 15 cm.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$48t+160$’: This is only 2(lw + lh) — it omits the two wh faces entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$318t+80$’: Equals 2(159t + 40) = 2(24t + 40 + 135t), accounting for all three pairs of faces. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘$1,968t+80$’: Far too large; likely from a multiplication error with the width. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$360t$’: Has no constant term, but lh = 40 is a constant, so the surface area must include a constant. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
